project.name: aquarium-cooling-controller
project.purpose: Aquarium cooling controller with local autonomous fan control, DS18B20 temperature sensing, PWM fan drive, tachometer monitoring, Wi-Fi and MQTT integration
project.primary_goal: Control aquarium water temperature by evaporative cooling using a 4-pin PWM fan, with all critical control logic running locally on the ESP32
project.secondary_goal: Use low fan speed to remove hot air from the covered aquarium when the air under the lid heats up due to the LED lighting
project.project_status: bench-verified local control firmware with water control, air-assist, target persistence, local fault policy, and broker-verified publish-only MQTT telemetry

system_context.environment: Covered aquarium with a dedicated fan opening in the lid and a separate air outlet opening
system_context.cooling_method: Fan blows onto/through the aquarium opening to increase evaporation and remove warm air
constraints: No local display or buttons are planned
Target temperature must persist across reboot and across Wi-Fi/MQTT outages
Critical control must never depend on MQTT or Wi-Fi availability
Low audible noise is more important than maximum cooling performance

reason: Enough resources for local control plus MQTT/Wi-Fi

temperature_sensors.assignment_rule: Sensors must be identified by ROM ID, not by bus order
reason: Stable role assignment across reboot and hardware changes

power.pd_trigger: 12V requested via USB-C PD trigger board
power.goal: Compact single-cable supply using an already available USB-C PD power supply

supporting_components.fan_pwm_driver_note: Final hardware design must ensure PWM signal is electrically compatible with 4-pin fan control input
supporting_components.fan_tach_note: Tach input must be handled as open-collector/open-drain style feedback

notes: Common ground between ESP32 and fan power supply is mandatory

behavior: Below target temperature: fan off or minimal support airflow
Slightly above target: low quiet cooling PWM
Further above target: gradually increase PWM
Critical temperature range: high PWM / emergency behavior
water_control.note: Bench-ready mapping is implemented; final tuning should use live aquarium data

air_assist.purpose: Remove hot air trapped under the lid due to the LED lighting
air_assist.behavior: Apply a minimum fan PWM when air temperature exceeds configured thresholds
air_assist.priority: Secondary to water cooling, but combined by max() logic

noise_priority.policy: Prefer quieter fan behavior over maximum cooling performance where safe
implication: Need start boost handling if required

fan_characterization_test.purpose: Characterize the specific physical fan before implementing the production controller
required_outputs: Minimum hold PWM while already spinning
Observations about acoustic behavior and unstable regions
fan_characterization_test.automation_goal: The test sketch should run fully automatically with no manual serial commands required
test_sequence: Search safe start PWM from standstill in small PWM steps
Measure increasing curve from safe start PWM to 100%
Search minimum hold PWM while fan is already spinning
Print summary and machine-usable curve data
serial_output_requirements: Summary of start PWM and hold PWM
Curve as C array initializer for reuse in production code

production_fault_model.core_definition: The fan is considered healthy only if measured RPM is plausible relative to commanded PWM, using the measured fan curve and a configured tolerance band
production_fault_model.explicit_user_decision: If measured RPM deviates outside the allowed tolerance from the expected RPM according to the fan curve, this is a fault

plausibility_method.reference_data: Measured PWM-to-RPM curve of the exact fan used in the system
plausibility_method.calculation: Linear interpolation between neighboring curve points
plausibility_method.evaluation_rule: Measured RPM must stay within expected RPM ± configured tolerance

tolerance_policy.initial_strategy: Use a fixed starting tolerance (example: ±12%) and refine after measurements
tolerance_policy.note: Very low PWM region may need wider tolerance or exclusion from plausibility checking

enable_conditions: Only evaluate plausibility above the minimum stable operating PWM
Pause plausibility evaluation for a settling period after PWM changes
Use multiple consecutive mismatches before latching a fault

fail_safe.philosophy: Local fail-safe behavior must protect cooling autonomy and surface real hardware faults

water_sensor_failure.policy: Enter fault handling because primary control variable is unavailable
water_sensor_failure.initial_recommendation: Drive fan to a defined safe fallback PWM and raise an alarm

air_sensor_failure.policy: Continue water-based cooling control, disable air-assist, raise warning diagnostic

fan_plausibility_failure.user_rule: Any confirmed RPM deviation outside the allowed tolerance band is a fault
fan_plausibility_failure.current_reaction: Report critical fan fault, keep locally computed PWM command, require service
fan_plausibility_failure.verification_status: Verified with missing tach feedback and deliberate RPM deviation by slowing the fan

wifi_mqtt_failure.policy: No impact on local cooling control; last persisted settings remain active

mqtt.design_status: Publish-only MQTT telemetry foundation is implemented and broker-verified; remote configuration and OTA remain planned

notes: MQTT is publish-only in the current firmware
Local cooling and fault handling remain authoritative on the ESP32
Broker credentials and local network configuration are intentionally not committed

mqtt.critical_rule: MQTT must never be required for the controller to keep cooling autonomously

style: Separate critical control logic from communication logic
Use structs or small helper modules where useful
production_code_principles: Explicit validation of remote parameters before persisting

codex_tasking.current_task: Publish-only MQTT telemetry foundation is implemented, broker-verified, and documented
next_expected_tasks: Tune water and air control behavior with live aquarium data
Finalize enclosure and wiring for the installed system
instructions_for_codex: Preserve the architectural rule that critical temperature control runs locally on the ESP32
Do not make MQTT or Wi-Fi a dependency for autonomous cooling
Treat the measured fan curve as the reference for plausibility checking
Implement fault detection conservatively with settling time and repeated mismatches
Prefer maintainable code over overly clever code

fan_characterization_phase: Automatic test sketch compiles on ESP32 Arduino
Sketch runs without manual serial commands
Sketch prints increasing and decreasing PWM-to-RPM curves
Sketch prints data reusable in production firmware
production_phase_high_level: Fan plausibility fault is detected using curve + tolerance
MQTT can observe current state without compromising autonomy
MQTT can later set non-critical parameters only after validation and persistence are implemented

known_open_points: Exact final tolerance percentage must be tuned after measurements
Final hardware-level PWM electrical interface to the selected fan must be validated in the built prototype
Curve should be verified once in free-air test and once in real installation if behavior changes significantly
non_goals_for_now: Using MQTT during fan characterization testing
